constraints: ¥n    ### General constraints / checks¥n¥n    - `dst` and `src` must both be `TileType::Vec`.¥n¥n    - `src` must use standard ND layout: row-major and non-fractal (`BLayout::RowMajor`, `SLayout::NoneBox`).¥n¥n    - `dst` must use one of the following non-fractal layouts:¥n      - ND layout (`BLayout::RowMajor`, `SLayout::NoneBox`), or¥n      - DN layout with exactly one column (`BLayout::ColMajor`, `SLayout::NoneBox`, `Cols == 1`).¥n¥n    - `dst` and `src` must use the same element type.¥n¥n    - Runtime valid-region checks:¥n      - `src.GetValidRow() != 0`¥n      - `src.GetValidCol() != 0`¥n      - `src.GetValidRow() == dst.GetValidRow()`¥n¥n    - Supported element types: `half`, `float`, `int32_t`, `int16_t`.¥n¥n    - The implementation accepts both ND output and DN output with `Cols == 1`; it is not limited to DN output.¥n¥n    - The current implementation path passes `tmp` into the backend call, but this document does not add extra `tmp` shape/layout constraints beyond what is explicitly enforced by the checked implementation.
cpp_intrinsic: template <typename TileDataOut, typename TileDataIn, typename TileDataTmp, typename... WaitEvents>¥nPTO_INST RecordEvent TROWPROD(TileDataOut &dst, TileDataIn &src, TileDataTmp &tmp, WaitEvents &... events);
examples: #include <pto/pto-inst.hpp>¥n¥nusing namespace pto;¥n¥nvoid example_auto() {¥n  using SrcT = Tile<TileType::Vec, float, 16, 16>;¥n  using DstT = Tile<TileType::Vec, float, 16, 1, BLayout::ColMajor>;¥n  using TmpT = Tile<TileType::Vec, float, 16, 16>;¥n  SrcT src;¥n  DstT dst;¥n  TmpT tmp;¥n  TROWPROD(dst, src, tmp);¥n}
illegal_cases: ¥n    - Illegal operand tuples, unsupported types, invalid layout combinations, or unsupported target-profile modes are rejected by the verifier or by the selected backend instruction set.¥n    - Programs must not rely on behavior outside the documented legal domain of this operation, even if one backend currently accepts it.
name: pto.trowprod
summary: Reduce each row by computing the product across columns.
target_profile_restrictions: ¥n    - The intrinsic signature requires an explicit `tmp` operand.
